description_long: This object is a hand tool featuring a straight, single-edged blade with a squared-off tip, likely made of dark metal. The blade is attached to a rounded, bulbous wooden handle that tapers smoothly and appears ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip. The handle is a rich brown color, contrasting with the black or dark gray blade, and has a subtle collar or ferrule at the junction with the blade for added strength. The blade is relatively thick and robust, suggesting it is designed for heavy-duty tasks such as woodworking, scraping, or prying. There are no visible decorative elements or engravings on either the blade or handle, giving it a utilitarian appearance. The overall construction appears solid and functional, suitable for use in workshops, garages, or urban maintenance settings.
description: A robust hand tool with a straight, dark metal blade and a rounded wooden handle, designed for heavy-duty cutting or scraping tasks. The tool has a utilitarian, undecorated appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rounded end of the wooden handle and the flat, wide face of the blade.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the full length of the straight blade and the ergonomic taper of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the broad, flat blade edge and the circular end of the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the blade's thickness and handle's profile.
attribute_handle_design: rounded, bulbous wooden handle with ergonomic taper and subtle collar
